channel.title: NPR Topics: National
channel.description: NPR coverage of national news, U.S. politics, elections, business, arts, culture, health and science, and technology. Subscribe to the NPR Nation RSS feed.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: Prison guards and inmates say New York's correctional system is broken
item.description: Prison guards in New York say the state's correctional system is in crisis. Both guards and inmates are pleading with the state to fix what they say is a broken system.
item.content:encoded: Prison guards in New York say the state's correctional system is in crisis. Both guards and inmates are pleading with the state to fix what they say is a broken system.
item.dc:creator: Emily Russell

item.title: Alleged gunman at White House Correspondents' Dinner appeared in court
item.description: The alleged gunman at the White House Correspondents' Dinner appeared in court Monday for his arraignment, where he faced charges of attempted assassination of the president, among others.
item.content:encoded: The alleged gunman at the White House Correspondents' Dinner appeared in court Monday for his arraignment, where he faced charges of attempted assassination of the president, among others.
item.dc:creator: Ryan Lucas

item.title: Supreme Court heard case on how to label risks of popular weed killer
item.description: How the Supreme Court rules could have implications for tens of thousands of lawsuits against Roundup maker Monsanto, which is now owned by Bayer.
item.content:encoded:
How the Supreme Court rules could have implications for tens of thousands of lawsuits against Roundup maker Monsanto, which is now owned by Bayer.

(Image credit: Jean-Francois Monier)
item.dc:creator: Carrie Johnson

item.title: Privacy and law enforcement clash as the Supreme Court wrestles with 'geofence' warrants
item.description: In oral arguments at the Supreme Court Monday, most of the justices aimed pointed questions at both sides, with the usual conservative-liberal alignments scrambled like an egg.
item.content:encoded:
In oral arguments at the Supreme Court Monday, most of the justices aimed pointed questions at both sides, with the usual conservative-liberal alignments scrambled like an egg.

(Image credit: Heather Diehl)
item.dc:creator: Nina Totenberg

item.title: Here's a look inside security at the White House Correspondents' Association Dinner
item.description: Saturday's shooting at the White House Correspondents' Association Dinner raised questions about how close the alleged gunman got to the president and what the Secret Service security looked like.
item.content:encoded:
Saturday's shooting at the White House Correspondents' Association Dinner raised questions about how close the alleged gunman got to the president and what the Secret Service security looked like.

(Image credit: Alex Brandon)
item.dc:creator: Tamara Keith

item.title: Florida's DeSantis unveils a voting map that could add to Trump's GOP redistricting
item.description: Florida's governor has called lawmakers to meet starting Tuesday. They'll consider a fast-track redistricting that could flip some House seats held by Democrats to Republicans.
item.content:encoded:
Florida's governor has called lawmakers to meet starting Tuesday. They'll consider a fast-track redistricting that could flip some House seats held by Democrats to Republicans.

(Image credit: Julia Demaree Nikhinson)
item.dc:creator: Douglas Soule

item.title: Iran's flurry of diplomacy continues in Russia, as Trump reviews Iran's latest proposal
item.description: Iran's foreign minister arrived in Russia on Monday, after a whirlwind weekend of diplomacy, seeking to gain political leverage and foreign backing as peace talks with the U.S. remain on hold.
item.content:encoded:
Iran's foreign minister arrived in Russia on Monday, after a whirlwind weekend of diplomacy, seeking to gain political leverage and foreign backing as peace talks with the U.S. remain on hold.

(Image credit: Dmitry Lovetsky)
item.dc:creator: NPR Staff

item.title: Trump says 'consequential' presidents face more danger after WHCA dinner shooting
item.description: After the shooting at the White House Correspondents' Association dinner in Washington, D.C., the White House was quick to praise law enforcement and call for unity, controlling the narrative early.
item.content:encoded: After the shooting at the White House Correspondents' Association dinner in Washington, D.C., the White House was quick to praise law enforcement and call for unity, controlling the narrative early.
item.dc:creator: A Martínez

item.title: Rep. Jamie Raskin shares his experience from the White House Correspondents' dinner
item.description: Steve Inskeep asks Maryland Democrat Jamie Raskin, ranking member of the House Judiciary Committee with oversight of the Secret Service, about experiencing Saturday's correspondents' dinner shooting.
item.content:encoded: Steve Inskeep asks Maryland Democrat Jamie Raskin, ranking member of the House Judiciary Committee with oversight of the Secret Service, about experiencing Saturday's correspondents' dinner shooting.
item.dc:creator: Steve Inskeep

item.title: Inflation is sucking the life out of teacher pay raises, report says
item.description: A new review of state education data shows teacher pay increases can't keep up with inflation and fewer students are enrolled in public schools.
item.content:encoded: A new review of state education data shows teacher pay increases can't keep up with inflation and fewer students are enrolled in public schools.
item.dc:creator: Cory Turner
